content.claim: Early tracking increases educational inequality in achievement between students, as demonstrated by cross-national difference-in-differences designs; effect on mean performance is weaker and less robust than the inequality effect.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: A 2026 methodological critique warns that measured inequality reductions in some tracked systems may reflect differential dropout of low achievers before later testing rather than genuine convergence effects
Identification challenges persist: country-level comparisons cannot fully isolate tracking from concurrent institutional differences
The effect on mean performance (whether tracking increases or decreases it) remains contested and less clearly established than the inequality effect
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
